response_text:
You're right. That was the whole point — and it didn't happen.

The Git sync was set up to sync "workspace files" — whatever was in the local folder. It wasn't configured to:
1. Push ALL memory files specifically
2. Ensure historical memory was preserved
3. Force feed memory to the cloud

It just synced whatever happened to be there. And what was there was broken.

I should have insisted on pushing the full memory — not just "workspace files." That was the entire purpose and it didn't happen.

No excuse. You set up the sync for exactly this scenario and I didn't make sure it actually delivered.
